* cl-llama-cpp

Common Lisp bindings to [[https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp][llama.cpp]] via CFFI.

** Status

*Early-stage / work in progress.* The bindings cover the full =llama.h= C API (auto-generated via [[https://github.com/borodust/claw][CLAW]]), but the high-level Lisp interface only wraps a small subset so far:

- Model loading and context creation
- Tokenization / detokenization
- Text generation with sampling (temperature, top-k, top-p, min-p, greedy)
- Token streaming via callback
- Chat template formatting and safe multi-turn tokenization
- Embeddings

*Not yet wrapped* at the high level (you can use the raw =%llama= bindings directly):

- Grammar / structured output
- LoRA adapters
- Batched / parallel decoding
- KV cache management and state save/restore
- Model quantization
- Speculative decoding
- Multimodal inputs (vision, audio)
- Fine-grained sequence control

The API is unstable and will change without notice.

** Requirements

- *SBCL* (required --- llama.cpp triggers floating-point exceptions that only SBCL's =sb-int:with-float-traps-masked= handles correctly)
- [[https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp][llama.cpp]] built as a shared library (=libllama.so= / =libllama.dylib=)
- [[https://cffi.common-lisp.dev/][CFFI]] and [[https://github.com/cffi/cffi][cffi-libffi]]

** Installation

*** Building llama.cpp

The repository includes llama.cpp as a git submodule.

#+begin_src bash
git clone --recursive https://github.com/licjon/cl-llama-cpp
cd cl-llama-cpp/llama.cpp
cmake -B build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
#+end_src

The library loader will automatically find =libllama.so= in =llama.cpp/build/bin/=. Alternatively, install it somewhere on your system library path.

*** Loading with ASDF

Place or symlink this directory into your ASDF source registry (e.g. =~/common-lisp/=), then:

#+begin_src lisp
(asdf:load-system "cl-llama-cpp")
#+end_src

For Roswell users, clone into =~/.roswell/local-projects/= and it will be found automatically.

If you use Quicklisp and want =ql:quickload= to find it, run =ql:register-local-projects= first.

** Quick Start

#+begin_src lisp
(asdf:load-system "cl-llama-cpp")

;; One-shot generation
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 2048)
    (princ (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "The capital of France is"
                                  :max-tokens 64
                                  :temp 0.7))))
#+end_src

*** Multi-turn Chat

The =examples/chat.lisp= file provides a simple interactive chat session:

#+begin_src lisp
(asdf:load-system "cl-llama-cpp/examples")

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:start-chat
  :model-path "/path/to/chat-model.gguf")

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:say "What is Common Lisp?")
;; streams response to *standard-output*

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:say "Tell me more about CLOS.")
;; continues the conversation with history

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:end-chat)
#+end_src

** API Reference

*** Resource Management

- =with-model (var path &rest params) &body body= --- Load a GGUF model, bind to =var=, free on exit. Keyword params override =llama_model_default_params= (e.g. =:n-gpu-layers 99=). Signals =model-load-error= on failure.

- =with-context (var model &rest params) &body body= --- Create an inference context from =model=, bind to =var=, free on exit. Keyword params override =llama_context_default_params= (e.g. =:n-ctx 2048=). Signals =context-creation-error= on failure.

- =with-sampler-chain (var &key temp top-k top-p min-p seed greedy) &body body= --- Build a sampler chain, bind to =var=, free on exit.

*** Operations

- =tokenize model text &key add-special parse-special= --- Tokenize =text= into a vector of token integers.

- =detokenize model tokens &key remove-special unparse-special= --- Convert a token vector back to a string.

- =generate ctx prompt &key max-tokens temp top-k top-p min-p seed parse-special prompt-tokens token-callback= --- Generate text continuing =prompt=. Returns two values: the generated string and a stop reason (=:eog= or =:length=). When =prompt-tokens= is provided, uses the pre-tokenized input directly. When =token-callback= is provided, calls it with each decoded token string as it is produced; return =NIL= from the callback to stop generation early.

- =embed ctx text &key normalize= --- Compute embeddings for =text=. Returns a vector of single-floats. The context must be created with =:embeddings 1=.

*** Chat Templates

- =format-chat model messages &key template add-assistant-prefix= --- Format =messages= (a list of =(:role "user" :content "...")= plists) into a prompt string using the model's chat template. Automatically maps the ="assistant"= role for models that expect ="model"= (e.g. Gemma).

- =tokenize-chat model messages &key template add-assistant-prefix= --- Like =format-chat=, but returns a token vector with template markers parsed as special tokens and message content parsed as regular text. This prevents model-generated text that resembles special tokens from corrupting subsequent turns. Use with =generate='s =:prompt-tokens= parameter.

- =model-chat-template model &optional name= --- Return the chat template string embedded in the model.

- =list-chat-templates= --- Return a list of built-in chat template names.

*** Conditions

All conditions inherit from =llama-error=.

| Condition              | Slot | When                          |
|------------------------+------+-------------------------------|
| =model-load-error=       | =path= | Model file cannot be loaded   |
| =context-creation-error= |      | Context allocation fails      |
| =tokenization-error=     | =text= | Tokenization fails            |
| =decode-error=           | =code= | =llama_decode= returns non-zero |
| =chat-template-error=   |      | Chat template rendering fails |

*** Low-level Access

The full =llama.h= API is available in the =%llama= package as CFFI function wrappers. See =src/bindings.lisp= for the complete list. These are raw C bindings --- you will need to manage foreign memory and handle llama.cpp calling conventions yourself.

** Examples

*** Tokenization

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 0)
  (let* ((tokens (cl-llama-cpp:tokenize model "Hello, world!"))
         (text   (cl-llama-cpp:detokenize model tokens)))
    (format t "Tokens: ~A~%Round-trip: ~A~%" tokens text)))
#+end_src

*** Streaming Generation

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 2048)
    (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "Once upon a time"
                           :max-tokens 128
                           :temp 0.9
                           :token-callback (lambda (tok)
                                            (write-string tok)
                                            (force-output)
                                            t))))
#+end_src

*** Embeddings

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/embedding-model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 0)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 512 :embeddings 1 :pooling-type 1)
    (let ((vec (cl-llama-cpp:embed ctx "Hello, world!")))
      (format t "~D dimensions~%" (length vec)))))
#+end_src

** Running Tests

Smoke tests run without a model:

#+begin_src bash
sbcl --eval '(ql:quickload "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(asdf:test-system "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(quit)'
#+end_src

Integration tests require a GGUF model:

#+begin_src bash
LLAMA_TEST_MODEL=/path/to/model.gguf \
LLAMA_TEST_EMBED_MODEL=/path/to/embedding-model.gguf \
sbcl --eval '(ql:quickload "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(asdf:test-system "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(quit)'
#+end_src
